metadata.title:
WOMEN'S RIGHT To VOTE


IS FAVORED BY LABOR

pages.raw_text:
The women will soon have a
right to vote in this state if the
members of the labor unions have
anything to say to it. At the state
convention at Walla Walla last
year they gave complete en-
dorsement of the woman's suffrage
program. This year at Houuiam
they promise to go a step farther,
and take an active part in securing
an amendment to the state consti-
tuition, insuring the right to vote to
the women of Washington.


William J. Coates, president of
the local central labor council, and
a delegate from that body and
from the local pressmen union
at Hoquiam, has introduced a reso-
luton on the subject at the con-
vention which is now in session.
The resolution stands good
chance of being passed. 1t is as
sured the woman's suffrage will
be one of the planks in the plat
form of the proposed new labor
party.
